title: Open Field test to assess spontaneous locomotion behavior in parkinsonian mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"29aqb","text":"The Open Field task is a simple sensorimotor test used to determine general activity levels, gross locomotor activity, and exploration habits in rodent models of CNS disorders.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the animal cage in the testing room to allow the animals to acclimate. 01:00:00
Place the animal in the testing boxes. The animals are transferred to the center of a 40cm by 40cm box in the quiet dark room.
Video record and record freely moving behavior in the testing cage. 00:05:00
Return the mouse to its cage.
The footage is then analyzed by an automated tracking system for the following parameters: distance moved, velocity, and time spent in pre-defined zones.
creator: Natalia Lopez-GonzalezdelRey
